visible_observations: A hairless pale humanoid crouches on long clawed hands and feet while numerous black serrated ribbon-like appendages erupt from the back and curl around the body in broad arcs.
The smooth elongated head has two deep black almond openings and no visible mouth; stitched seams, carved circular marks, exposed corded neck tissue, and fine scars cover the gray skin.
A low stalking posture, forward hands, head tilt, and encircling hooked appendages create watchful, invasive pressure around an otherwise slender body.
strengths: The pale faceless body against multiple black blade-ribbons creates a distinctive alien body plan with strong color and shape separation.
Scars, sutures, carved marks, and exposed tissue make alteration and bodily consequence visible without relying on clothing or floating symbols.
The head tilt and asymmetrical crouch introduce an attentive presence rather than a neutral specimen pose.
defects: The many similarly tapered appendages form an even decorative halo and do not visibly distinguish locomotion, attack, defense, or restraint functions.
The slender smooth gray humanoid and black eye voids invoke a familiar generic alien or faceless-creature vocabulary before the smaller body marks establish individuality.
Because no appendage visibly contacts a target, the surrounding arcs can read as display or atmosphere as much as immediate attack.
uncertainties: Unaided inspection cannot determine whether the black appendages are controlled limbs, parasites, shadows, blades, or a separate organism.
The body scale, combat method, and purpose of the circular carved marks remain visually uncertain.

visible_observations: Three black alternatives show a crouched humanoid surrounded by many short scything ribbons, a tall upright figure enclosed by four long downward blade-limbs, and a low crawling figure paired with one enormous overhead crescent appendage.
The candidates materially vary height, number and direction of appendages, center of gravity, negative space, and implied locomotion.
strengths: Candidate A creates a radial multi-limbed stalker, candidate B a suspended or ceremonial upright entity, and candidate C a fast ground predator with one dominant arc; the silhouettes are genuinely different.
Candidate A maintains several open gaps between body and ribbons, preserving both the humanoid core and surrounding threat.
defects: Candidate A has many competing curved tips with no clear primary action line, candidate B is close to a conventional elongated faceless figure, and candidate C lets one appendage overwhelm the small crawling body.
The alternatives alter appendage count and behavior enough that the underlying species and combat grammar are not stable across the sheet.
uncertainties: The silhouettes do not reveal whether the appendages attach anatomically or whether their curves represent one frozen motion rather than a resting body plan.

visible_observations: A smooth pale head tilts toward the viewer with two large black openings, no mouth, stitched cranial seams, small scars, and a narrow jaw-like lower taper.
The shoulders and hands stay low beneath the head while black appendages frame it from behind, giving the still face an alert forward attention.
strengths: The absence of mouth, unblinking voids, tilted head, and surgically marked skin form a coherent nonhuman attention mechanism rather than a hidden humanoid expression.
The eye openings remain the uncontested focal point and direct the creature toward the viewer.
defects: Fixed black openings have no pupil direction, lid tension, brow movement, mouth, or asymmetry, so attentive curiosity, dissociation, hunger, fear, and predation remain visually interchangeable.
The smooth oval head and black eyes are a familiar alien shorthand, while the more individual seams and marks carry little emotional information.
uncertainties: The unaided crop does not establish whether the head tilt is curious, cautious, wounded, submissive, or preparing to strike.

visible_observations: At native size, a small pale crouched body, bright oval head, long front limbs, and a pinwheel of black curved appendages remain visible.
The eye openings, scars, sutures, carved marks, claws, neck anatomy, and distinctions among individual ribbons collapse into the dark background and circular mass.
strengths: The pale central body within a black radial appendage field forms an unusual and quickly recognizable icon.
The low posture and multiple hooked arcs retain a sense of encirclement and nonhuman movement.
defects: The appendages merge into a decorative swirl, making their count, attachment, and attack direction unreadable.
The body becomes a small pale center rather than an independently legible creature, and the faceless attention mechanism does not survive.
uncertainties: The native image alone does not establish whether players would read a many-limbed predator, a pale figure inside a vortex, or a static radial emblem.
